narrative: For FY2022-23, the apparent leaders on total POSH complaints are JSW Steel and Jindal Stainless, both reporting zero complaints in the standardised table, while Tata Steel is the laggard with 38 complaints and SAIL sits mid-pack with 8. However, the year is weak for benchmarking because upheld complaints are missing for all four companies, and Tata’s FY2022-23 figure is flagged as consolidated, including Indian and international operations, while the other company-years are standalone. The more defensible conclusion is that Tata had the highest reported case load, not necessarily the weakest system. Higher reporting may reflect greater scale, broader employee/worker coverage, more mature grievance routes and stronger speak-up culture. SAIL’s own FY2023 materiality discussion identifies human rights and ethics/code of conduct as material governance/social risks, indicating that POSH sits within a broader compliance and reputational-risk frame for large steel operators.
